audit_meta.head_summary: [audit P1-P5] Wire net trust boundary + audio cursor + README + cleanup

pass_1_findings_closed.verification: grep src/net/transport.ts:148 shows parseNetMessage(parsed) wired; protocol.test.ts +60 LOC of strengthening tests pass
grep src/net/sync.ts:91-119 shows try/catch around applyNetSnapshot; sync.test.ts +38 LOC verifies no throw escapes interpolateInto for malformed snapshots
grep src/state/save.ts:306 + src/main.ts:359 both call resetAudioDrainCursor; save.test.ts +26 LOC verifies post-restore audio cursor reset
ls README.md → 5003 bytes; contains quick-start, architecture diagram, tech stack, doc index
boot-snapshot.md:12 grep shows both '500 KB cap' (app code) and '~729 KB' (incl Pixi)
grep NET_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MS in src/constants.ts returns 0 hits; knip total exports dropped 46→42
grep BOND_LINE_WIDTH/BOND_GLOW_INTENSITY/AUDIO_MASTER_VOLUME_DB in src/constants.ts returns 0 hits; knip total exports dropped 46→42

src/net/protocol.ts:99-149 grep shows KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES allowlist + schemaVersion check + numeric playerId/color + string godlyId; protocol.test.ts adds 8 new strengthening test cases all passing

findings.description: Wire allowlist of GameAction discriminants is hand-maintained alongside the type union in world.ts; no compile-time guarantee they stay synchronized.
findings.details: Pass-1 fix `561e37ce` introduced `KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES` (19 string literals) in protocol.ts as a closed allowlist for `INTENT.action.type` validation. The structural source-of-truth lives in `src/state/world.ts:GameAction` (a discriminated union over 19 variants). If a future PR adds a new action variant to `GameAction` without updating the protocol.ts set, valid INTENT payloads from a correctly-typed peer are silently rejected — manifesting as 'peer's input dropped' with a console.warn that's easy to miss. The audit code comment frames this as 'deliberate' (wire-allowlist independent of in-process type widening), which is defensible but creates a maintainability trap. Karpathy K1 (silent assumption) — easy to add new GameAction without remembering the wire side.

signals: no compile-time link between the two

recommendation.description: Two viable patterns: (a) `as const` array in world.ts that derives the union type via `(typeof ARR)[number]['type']`, then protocol.ts imports the runtime array and converts to Set — single source. (b) Compile-time exhaustiveness check via a `satisfies` block that asserts `KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES` is a `Set<GameAction['type']>` — if union widens, the literal-typed set narrows and tsc errors. Pattern (b) is lower-risk (no runtime change). Either is Micro-tier (~5K tokens).

verification.details: Compile-time: introduce a deliberate stub GameAction variant in a test branch; expect tsc to fail at the protocol.ts allowlist check (pattern b) OR the protocol.ts import to widen automatically (pattern a). Revert the stub; existing 740 tests stay green.

findings.description: Pass-1 fix 3c8630d7 introduced a state→render dependency edge. Pinpointed and documented but the only such edge in the codebase.
findings.details: Before Pass 1, all imports in `src/state/` referenced other `src/state/`, `src/game/`, `src/physics/`, `src/types.ts` modules — the state layer was render-agnostic by convention. The Pass-1 fix added `import { resetAudioDrainCursor } from '../render/audioManager.ts'` at save.ts:57. The audit fix comment frames this as 'deliberate and pinpointed' and notes the imported function is pure module-level state reset with no AudioContext involvement, so it's safe to call from test paths that never initialize audio. The architectural concern is precedent: future work may slip more render imports into state with this as cover.

recommendation.description: Two cleanups possible: (1) move `resetAudioDrainCursor` (and the future-equivalent cursor reset on world.tick discontinuity) into a `src/state/audioCursor.ts` reset-event publisher; subscribers in render/audioManager register themselves at module init. Restores the state-render boundary. (2) Accept the precedent and codify it: document in CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md that pure render-layer reset functions may be imported from state. Decision is project-style; not urgent.

verification.details: If accepting precedent: add a one-line policy note. If reverting: a follow-up PR replaces direct imports with publisher/subscriber + adds a regression test that grep `'../render/'` in src/state/ returns 0.

findings.description: parseNetMessage accepts NETSNAPSHOT with snapshot.schemaVersion=undefined for the existing test-double pattern. Production payloads always carry schemaVersion=1, so a peer-sent `{snapshot:{}}` bypasses the version check.
findings.details: Pass-1 fix added schemaVersion validation to parseNetMessage at protocol.ts:140-146, with a deliberate carve-out: `if (schemaVersion !== undefined && schemaVersion !== WIRE_SCHEMA_VERSION)`. The undefined branch exists to keep existing protocol.test.ts cases compatible (`snapshot: {}` test fixtures). However, a peer can now send `{kind:'NETSNAPSHOT', snapshotSeq:1, snapshot:{}}` and bypass the version gate at the wire. Downstream, `applyNetSnapshot` throws on `schemaVersion !== 1` (save.ts:328); the Pass-1 try/catch guard in sync.ts catches the throw and skips the apply. Net blast radius: a malicious peer can cost the client one frame per malformed snapshot (visible as a momentary render glitch); cannot crash. Defense-in-depth still holds because of the e698a17a guard, but the wire validator's permissive carve-out is a latent gap.

signals: test fixture pattern: `{ kind: 'NETSNAPSHOT', snapshotSeq: 1, snapshot: {} }` used at protocol.test.ts:83
applyNetSnapshot throw guarded by try/catch at sync.ts:107

ralph.hypothesis: A peer sending `{kind:'NETSNAPSHOT', snapshotSeq:1, snapshot:{}}` bypasses parseNetMessage, then crashes-and-recovers via the sync.ts try/catch — one dropped frame per malformed message.
ralph.minimal_repro: Construct the payload above. Assert parseNetMessage returns NOT-null. Assert ClientSync.receive accepts it. Assert interpolateInto does NOT throw (already covered by sync.test.ts new test). The chain works as designed but the validator is permissive.
ralph.fix_design: Tighten the validator: require `schemaVersion === WIRE_SCHEMA_VERSION` (no undefined carve-out). Update the 1 existing protocol.test.ts case at line 83 from `snapshot: {}` to `snapshot: { schemaVersion: 1 }`. Both are single-line changes.
ralph.verification_plan: After tightening, the wire-empty-snapshot test should return null (assertion flip). All other tests stay green.

recommendation.description: Remove the `schemaVersion !== undefined` carve-out at protocol.ts:145; update the protocol.test.ts NETSNAPSHOT fixture to include schemaVersion=1. Micro-tier; ~2K tokens. The defense-in-depth guard in sync.ts remains as belt-and-suspenders but the wire becomes properly tight.

verification.details: Updated protocol.test.ts asserts `{kind:'NETSNAPSHOT', snapshotSeq:1, snapshot:{}}` returns null; `{kind:'NETSNAPSHOT', snapshotSeq:1, snapshot:{schemaVersion:1}}` returns NOT-null.

findings.description: [CARRY-FORWARD] Pass-2 audit commit 5750cd6 itself touched 9 src/ + 4 root files — concentrated in net+state+render+root docs. K3 self-check passes for this commit.
findings.details: Pass-1 finding was the workflow-level observation. Self-check on the Pass-2 fix commit: scope was `[audit P1-P5]` (priority-tag style), files touched: src/net/* (transport.ts, protocol.ts, protocol.test.ts, sync.ts, sync.test.ts) + src/state/* (save.ts, save.test.ts) + src/main.ts + src/constants.ts + 4 root docs. Each file touch is justified in the commit body. No collateral creep — every file change traces to a Pass-1 finding ID. K3 sweep for other recent priorities still recommended per Pass 1.

signals: every file touch documented in commit body with finding ID cross-ref

recommendation.description: Same as Pass 1 — Pass-2 audit commit itself is scope-clean; broader sweep across S<N> priorities still recommended.
